name: startup_blocks_until_clean
description: Startup reconciliation must run before any trading task and abort boot on mismatch
prompt: Write the startup reconciliation function for TradeOS that blocks trading from starting until positions match the broker. If any mismatch is found, startup must abort — no trading tasks should be created. Show how reconcile_on_startup() integrates with the main() coroutine.

expectations.text: reconcile_on_startup() is awaited before any asyncio.create_task() call
Uses asyncio.to_thread(kite.positions) — not kite.positions() directly in the event loop
Raises ReconciliationMismatchError (or SystemExit/equivalent) when mismatches found — does not silently continue
Logs structured event at CRITICAL level on mismatch (not just WARNING)
Returns cleanly (True or None) when no mismatches — trading proceeds normally

name: compare_broker_vs_local
description: Comparison algorithm must correctly identify qty_mismatch, ghost_position, and missing_local cases
prompt: Write the position comparison function for TradeOS that takes broker positions (from kite.positions()['net']) and local positions (from shared_state['position_state']) and returns a list of mismatches. Show examples of: a qty_mismatch (broker has 10, local has 5), a ghost_position (broker has 10, local has 0), and a missing_local (broker has 0, local has 10).

expectations.text: Detects qty_mismatch: broker_qty != local_qty when both are non-zero
Detects ghost_position: broker_qty != 0 but no local record (local_qty == 0)
Detects missing_local: local_qty != 0 but broker_qty == 0
Ghost positions are flagged as severity='critical', other mismatches as severity='warning'
Returns empty list (not None or exception) when positions are clean

name: mismatch_locks_instrument_only
description: A mismatch on one instrument must lock ONLY that instrument, not halt all trading
prompt: Show how TradeOS handles a position mismatch on RELIANCE when INFY is trading cleanly. The mismatch should lock RELIANCE but INFY signals must continue. Show the instrument lock in shared_state['locked_instruments'] and the signal_processor gate that checks it.

expectations.text: locked_instruments is a set in shared_state — only the mismatched token is added
signal_processor checks 'token in shared_state[locked_instruments]' and skips locked instruments
Unlocked instruments (INFY) continue to generate signals during the lock
Sends Telegram WARNING alert with instrument name and mismatch details
Lock persists — not cleared until reconciliation confirms clean or manual unlock

name: auto_adjust_disabled_by_default
description: Auto-adjust must be gated by config flag and disabled by default; manual mode is default
prompt: Write the mismatch handler for TradeOS that applies either manual lock mode or auto-adjust mode depending on config. The default (when config key is absent) must be manual mode. Show both code paths and demonstrate that auto-adjust NEVER applies to ghost positions even when enabled.

expectations.text: Checks config.get('reconciliation', {}).get('auto_adjust', False) — defaults to False
Manual mode: locks instrument, logs warning, sends alert — does NOT modify local state
Auto-adjust mode: updates local quantity to match broker — does NOT lock the instrument
Ghost positions always use manual mode regardless of auto_adjust setting
Auto-adjust logs a WARNING (not INFO) — it's an anomaly correction, not a normal event

name: ghost_position_close_protocol
description: Ghost positions must be logged CRITICAL, sent to Telegram, and never auto-closed
prompt: Write the ghost position handler for TradeOS. A ghost position means the broker shows an open position that the TradeOS system has no record of. Show the full handling: detection, logging, Telegram alert, locking, and explain why auto-closing is dangerous.

expectations.text: Logs CRITICAL event 'ghost_position_detected' with instrument and broker_qty
Sends Telegram CRITICAL alert (not just warning) for ghost position
Locks the instrument in shared_state['locked_instruments']
Marks position in shared_state with is_ghost=True flag — does NOT place any close order
Explains why auto-closing is dangerous: could be a hedge or manual safety trade; unexpected P&L impact

name: scheduled_recon_every_30_min
description: Scheduled reconciler must run every 30 minutes and never halt trading on error
prompt: Write the scheduled_reconciler task for TradeOS that runs position reconciliation every 30 minutes. If a reconciliation call fails (e.g. network error), it must log the error and wait for the next cycle — it must never crash or halt trading for other instruments.

expectations.text: Uses asyncio.sleep(1800) — exactly 30 minutes between reconciliation cycles
try/except wraps the reconcile call — errors are logged but do not crash the scheduled reconciler
Does NOT raise on mismatch during scheduled recon — locks instrument and continues
Uses asyncio.to_thread(kite.positions) inside the reconcile call — blocking I/O rule
Logs 'reconciliation_start' and 'reconciliation_complete' events with trigger='scheduled'

name: post_disruption_pauses_signal_processor
description: Post-disruption reconciliation must pause signal_processor via recon_in_progress flag
prompt: Write the post-disruption reconciliation flow for TradeOS that runs after a WebSocket reconnect. During reconciliation, the signal_processor must not process any ticks. Show both: (1) the function that sets shared_state['recon_in_progress'] and runs reconciliation, and (2) the gate inside signal_processor that checks the flag.

expectations.text: Sets shared_state['recon_in_progress'] = True BEFORE calling reconcile_positions()
Uses try/finally to ensure recon_in_progress is reset to False even if reconciliation throws
signal_processor checks shared_state.get('recon_in_progress', False) and skips ticks when True
Logs 'post_disruption_reconciliation_start' and 'post_disruption_reconciliation_complete'
After reconciliation completes, signal_processor resumes automatically — no manual restart needed

name: lock_persists_across_reconnect
description: Instrument lock must survive WebSocket reconnect and task restart — only cleared by successful reconciliation
prompt: Explain and demonstrate that instrument locks in TradeOS persist across WebSocket reconnects and task restarts. Show the shared_state['locked_instruments'] set lifecycle: when it is initialised (once in main()), when locks are added (on mismatch), when they are cleared (only on successful reconciliation), and why WebSocket reconnect does NOT clear them.

expectations.text: locked_instruments is initialised once in main() / _init_shared_state() — never reset on reconnect
WebSocket reconnect does NOT clear locked_instruments — reconnect handler does not touch the lock set
Locks are cleared only by _clear_lock_if_resolved() after a clean reconciliation
After reconnect, system runs post-disruption reconciliation — only if positions match does it unlock
Explains that a mismatch before reconnect may still exist after reconnect — must verify, not assume
